Signatures & Support — Extended Analysis
Brisbane ePetitions · Stephen Hanley · Draft v0.1
← Main analysis
Community feedback · Extended analysis · Draft v0.1
How much support do petitions get — and does active transport punch its weight?
A question from the community asked whether the analysis accounts for how many signatures petitions receive, and how active transport compares to other categories. This page extends the main analysis to answer that directly. All figures use the same dataset: 1,244 Brisbane City Council ePetitions scraped May 2026.
Key finding
Active transport petitions have gathered 68,735 signatures over 13 years — the second largest total of any theme. But the per-petition average (143 signatures) is lower than development objections (226) and public transport (199). What active transport has that others don't is sustained, consistent growth year on year — no other theme has grown as reliably across the full 13-year period.
68,735
total active transport signatures over 13 years
143
average signatures per active transport petition
57
median — most AT petitions are hyperlocal with modest counts
Theme comparison
Signatures by theme
Three ways to compare petition support across themes. Total signatures reflects mobilisation at scale. Average signatures reflects the typical petition. Petition count reflects sustained, repeated civic engagement over time.
Methodology note
Petitions are classified by keyword matching and may appear in multiple theme counts. Active transport uses the narrow definition here (478 petitions) for consistency with slide 1 of the main analysis. The expanded definition (510 petitions including traffic calming and liveability) is explained on slide 3 of the main analysis.

The development objection effect
Development objections generate the highest average signatures per petition (226) despite being a relatively small category (133 petitions). People mobilise intensely to stop specific developments near them — producing high local signature counts. It is the clearest expression of the NIMBY dynamic in the dataset.
Top petitions · By theme · Ranked by signatures
The petitions that resonated most
The five highest-signature petitions in each theme — the asks that mobilised the most people. Not always the ones that got the best response from council.
